title: 20 Best Dishes for Muscle Gain and Weight Loss | High-Protein Recipes | Eati
introduction: Building muscle and losing fat at the same time requires one thing above all: consistently eating enough protein while keeping total calories controlled. These 20 dishes each deliver 34–48g of protein in 280–490 calories — the macro profile that supports body recomposition whether you're cutting, maintaining, or slowly bulking. Every recipe links to a full breakdown with ingredients, step-by-step instructions, macro tables, and meal-prep tips.

sections.heading: Why These Dishes Support Muscle Gain and Fat Loss
sections.content:
Every dish on this list shares the same nutritional profile: high protein, controlled calories, and a macro balance that supports both building muscle and losing fat. Here's why that combination works:

Protein is the non-negotiable:
Muscle tissue is built from amino acids supplied by dietary protein. Research from the Journal of the International Society of Sports Nutrition shows that 1.6–2.2g of protein per kg of body weight per day maximises muscle protein synthesis during both bulking and cutting phases. For a 75kg person, that's 120–165g of protein daily. Every dish on this list contributes 34–48g per serving — making it easy to hit that target across 3–4 meals.

Calorie control for simultaneous fat loss:
The dishes range from 280–490 calories per serving, well under the 600–800 calories often found in restaurant equivalents. That gap is your deficit. Eat these dishes, stay in a 300–500 calorie daily deficit, and your body has no choice but to break down stored fat for energy — while the protein preserves your muscle mass.

Carbs and fat at the right level:
Carbohydrates fuel training sessions and replenish glycogen post-workout. Fat supports hormone production including testosterone. Neither should be eliminated — both are included in appropriate amounts in every dish on this list. The key is that protein is always the anchor, and total calories stay controlled.

Tracking makes the difference:
The fastest way to build muscle and lose fat simultaneously is to know exactly what you're eating. Use Eati to log these dishes — the app calculates macros instantly from a text description or barcode scan, so you always know where you stand. Download Eati on the App Store and start tracking today.

sections.heading: How to Build a Full Day of Eating Around These Dishes
sections.content:
A complete muscle-gain and fat-loss meal plan uses 3–4 of these dishes per day, spaced 3–4 hours apart.

Sample daily plan for a 75kg person targeting fat loss (2,000 cal, 160g protein):

Breakfast (goal: 35–40g protein, 300–400 cal):
• [Egg White Omelet with Turkey & Spinach](/blog/egg-white-omelet-turkey-spinach-recipe) — 280 cal, 38g protein

Lunch (goal: 40–45g protein, 400–500 cal):
• [Grilled Chicken, Rice & Broccoli](/blog/grilled-chicken-rice-broccoli-recipe) — 485 cal, 48g protein

Afternoon snack (goal: 20–25g protein, 150–200 cal):
• Cottage cheese with berries — 200 cal, 22g protein (separate recipe)

Dinner (goal: 35–40g protein, 350–420 cal):
• [Lemon Herb Baked Salmon](/blog/lemon-herb-baked-salmon-recipe) — 390 cal, 40g protein

Day total: ~1,955 cal, ~168g protein. That's a 300–500 cal deficit for most people while fully covering protein needs.

Sample plan for muscle gain (2,600 cal, 180g protein):
• Breakfast: [Protein Pancakes with Banana](/blog/protein-pancakes-banana-recipe) — 380 cal, 36g protein
• Lunch: [Tuna Steak with Quinoa](/blog/tuna-steak-quinoa-recipe) — 420 cal, 45g protein
• Pre-workout: [Chicken Breast & Sweet Potato](/blog/chicken-breast-sweet-potato-recipe) — 420 cal, 40g protein
• Dinner: [Lean Beef & Vegetable Bowl](/blog/lean-beef-vegetable-bowl-recipe) — 430 cal, 42g protein
• Evening: Greek yogurt with protein powder — ~300 cal, 40g protein
Day total: ~1,950 cal from dishes + ~650 cal from snacks = 2,600 cal, ~203g protein.

midArticleCta: Track your macros for every dish on this list in seconds. Log meals by text, photo, or barcode in Eati and always know where your protein and calories stand.
conclusion: Pick 3–4 dishes from this list, batch cook on the weekend, and track every serving in Eati. Consistent protein at the right calorie level is the entire game — and these 20 dishes make it easy to hit your numbers without overthinking food.
metaTitle: 20 Best Dishes for Muscle Gain and Weight Loss (High-Protein Recipes)
metaDescription: 20 high-protein dishes for muscle gain and fat loss — each with 34–48g protein and full calorie, fat, protein, and carb counts. Includes meal plans and tracking tips.
targetKeyword: best dishes for muscle gain and weight loss

faqs.question: What foods are best for muscle gain and weight loss at the same time?
faqs.answer: Foods that are high in protein and moderate in calories are best for simultaneous muscle gain and fat loss. The top options include chicken breast, salmon, tuna, lean beef, shrimp, egg whites, turkey, and cod — all of which appear in this list with 34–48g of protein per serving in under 500 calories.

faqs.question: How much protein do I need to build muscle while losing fat?
faqs.answer: Research supports 1.6–2.2g of protein per kg of body weight per day to maximise muscle protein synthesis while in a calorie deficit. For a 75kg person, that's 120–165g daily. Spreading that across 3–4 meals of 35–45g each covers the target — which is exactly what each dish on this list is designed to support.

faqs.question: Can I lose fat and build muscle eating 400–500 calories per meal?
faqs.answer: Yes. A 400–500 calorie meal with 40g+ of protein is the ideal macro profile for body recomposition. It provides enough calories to fuel training and recovery while staying in a daily calorie deficit of 300–500 calories when meals are spaced throughout the day. Track total daily intake with Eati to confirm your deficit.

faqs.question: Which dish has the most protein per calorie?
faqs.answer: The egg white omelet with turkey and spinach has the best protein-to-calorie ratio at 280 calories and 38g of protein — about 13.6g of protein per 100 calories. Baked cod with roasted vegetables is second at 310 cal / 36g protein (11.6g per 100 cal).

faqs.question: How many meals per day should I eat for muscle gain?
faqs.answer: Most research supports 3–4 protein-containing meals per day for maximum muscle protein synthesis. Each meal should contain at least 25–40g of protein to fully stimulate the anabolic response. More frequent, smaller meals offer no measurable advantage as long as total daily protein and calories are met.
